title: Capstone Mastery Contract and Evidence Architecture · Claim Repair Board
summary: A preserved decision record for capstone mastery contract and evidence architecture contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in capstone competency and evidence architecture and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Map every claimed competency to prerequisite evidence, new transfer task, artifact, verification, counterexample, and defense question.
Declare prior code, templates, libraries, collaborators, generated assistance, external services, and personal contribution with verification boundaries.
Evaluate whether proposed evidence is authentic, observable, sufficient, reproducible, current, and resistant to superficial completion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Competency Claim Map
steps.instruction:
debug case — An ambitious proposal lists technologies but cannot map independent competency claims to authentic artifacts, counterexamples, contribution limits, or unseen questions. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Map every claimed competency to prerequisite evidence, new transfer task, artifact, verification, counterexample, and defense question.

Inspect the Competency Claim Map trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Competency Claim Map.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map every claimed competency to prerequisite evidence, new transfer task, artifact, verification, counterexample, and defense question. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Competency Claim Map, keep Competency Claim Map connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Competency Claim Map, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Competency Claim Map, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Competency Claim Map precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Competency Claim Map, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: An ambitious proposal lists technologies but cannot map independent competency claims to authentic artifacts, counterexamples, contribution limits, or unseen questions. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Map every claimed competency to prerequisite evidence, new transfer task, artifact, verification, counterexample, and defense question.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Competency Claim Map, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One complex artifact automatically demonstrates every skill used to create it.

stimulus.title: Competency Claim Map case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map every claimed competency to prerequisite evidence, new transfer task, artifact, verification, counterexample, and defense question. Artifact under review: capstone competency and evidence architecture.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Competency Claim Map, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the inspect the failure record evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Assessment Stop Rule
steps.instruction:

The debug variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment.
